articles.title: Scott Mills Loses His Radio Job
articles.summary: Scott Mills is a radio host who worked for the BBC. He was recently fired from his job on BBC Radio 2. The BBC said he is no longer working for them, but they did not say exactly why. Mills had been hosting the breakfast show on Radio 2 since January 2025. He finished his show one day and said he would be back, but the next morning, another host, Gary Davies, took over. The news was first reported by a newspaper called The Mirror. Lorna Clarke, a boss at the BBC, sent an email to staff saying this news might be surprising and shocking to everyone. Jeremy Vine, another radio host, said he was also surprised and had no more information. Mills has worked for the BBC for many years on different radio and TV shows.

articles.title: NASA's New Trip to the Moon
articles.summary: NASA is sending astronauts back to the Moon with the Artemis II mission. This mission will help prepare for future Moon landings and building a base there. The Moon has important resources like water, metals, and rare elements that can be used for living and exploring space. Water is especially useful because it can be turned into air to breathe and fuel for rockets. NASA wants to go back to learn more and use these resources, unlike the old Apollo missions that were about racing to space. This new journey involves many people and costs a lot of money, but it aims to help humans live and work on the Moon someday.

articles.title: Car Finance Compensation: Getting Money Back for Unfair Deals
articles.summary: Millions of drivers in the UK will soon learn how to claim money back for car finance deals that were not fair. The Financial Conduct Authority (FCA), a group that makes rules for money matters, is announcing a plan today. This plan will help about 14 million people who bought cars with finance between 2007 and 2024. Many of these deals had problems like hidden commissions or wrong information, making people pay too much. On average, each person might get around £700 back. The FCA wants to make it easy to claim without going to court, but some lenders might challenge this in court, which could delay payments. This issue has been going on for a long time and involves a lot of money, with billions of pounds set aside for compensation. It shows how important it is for car buyers to understand their finance agreements.

articles.title: North Korea Tests a New Missile Engine
articles.summary: North Korea tested a new engine for missiles that could reach the United States. Their leader, Kim Jong Un, watched the test and said it makes their military stronger. The engine uses solid fuel, which makes missiles easier to move and hide. Some experts think North Korea might be exaggerating about how good the engine is because they did not share all the details. North Korea has been testing missiles for years, and this test is part of their plan to build more weapons. Other countries are watching closely because this could make it harder to stop missiles. The test shows North Korea is trying to improve its weapons, but we do not know exactly when or where it happened.

articles.title: A Missile Attack and Worries About Ships
articles.summary: A group called the Houthis in Yemen fired missiles at Israel. This makes people worry they might start attacking ships in the Red Sea. The Red Sea is a busy waterway where many ships carry goods like oil and toys around the world. If ships can't use it, they have to go a longer way, which costs more money and could make things more expensive for everyone. The Houthis are supported by Iran, and they are part of a bigger group that includes other fighters in places like Lebanon. Israel says it is ready for a fight on many fronts. Experts say blocking the Red Sea could hurt the global economy and make oil prices go up. This is important because it affects how things are shipped and traded internationally.

articles.title: Art Thieves Steal Famous Paintings in Italy
articles.summary: In Italy, near the city of Parma, a museum was robbed by four masked men. They stole three valuable paintings by famous artists: Renoir, Cézanne, and Matisse. The thieves broke into the museum, took the paintings in just three minutes, and escaped by climbing over a fence. An alarm went off, which stopped them from stealing more art. The paintings are worth millions of euros, with one by Renoir alone worth €6 million. Police are now investigating the theft. This museum is called the Magnani Rocca Foundation, and it was set up after an art collector named Luigi Magnani died. Art thefts like this have happened before, such as at the Louvre in Paris last year.
